summary: Frontend-only verification of /business-card route and SPA fallback config. All 9 requested checks passed: page renders with correct card-front/card-back content, print-card-btn is enabled, card-back-home links to /, vercel.json + _redirects present and correctly formatted, /random-non-existent-path returns HTTP 200 (SPA behavior via dev server), landing page unaffected.

issues: React Router logs warning 'No routes matched location' and renders empty body for unknown paths. Not blocking (dev server + Vercel rewrite still return HTTP 200), but adding a catch-all <Route path='*'> with a 404 page or redirect to '/' would improve UX in production.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Add a catch-all <Route path='*' element={<Navigate to='/' replace />} /> (or a proper NotFound page) in App.js so unknown paths render meaningful content instead of a blank body.
critical_code_review_comments: vercel.json rewrite `/(.*)` -> `/` is correct and will serve index.html for all client routes on Vercel.
public/_redirects `/*  /index.html  200` is correct Netlify-style SPA fallback.
BusinessCard.js uses window.print() correctly guarded via button click handler (no auto-invoke).
All required data-testid attributes present: card-toolbar, card-back-home, print-card-btn, card-front, card-back.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Only local dev server (http://localhost:3000) was tested per instructions; Vercel production was NOT tested since the SPA rewrite fix only takes effect after redeploy. window.print() was intentionally NOT invoked to avoid hanging the native print dialog. Curl checks: /business-card -> 200, / -> 200, /random-non-existent-path -> 200.
rca of the issue: Original 'cannot open /business-card' was caused by Vercel serving unknown paths as 404 because no SPA rewrite existed. Fix: vercel.json with rewrites `/(.*)` -> `/` and _redirects `/*  /index.html  200` for Netlify parity. Both files verified present and correctly formatted.
